event_description: An airborne camera observes a red car parked in an asphalt parking lot. The EO view shows a stationary vehicle indistinguishable from others in terms of operational status. The IR view reveals a distinct bright (hot) spot over the engine compartment, indicating recent operation (residual heat). Additionally, a dark (cold) moving entity, likely a person, is seen walking toward the car in the IR view.

qa.question: In the early EO segment, which cue pairing is visible for the object?

options.A: the object with parked car state with residual heat; cold moving person nearby
options.B: the object with cold moving person; parked car state with residual heat nearby
options.C: the object with parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles; asphalt markings or curb edges nearby
options.D: the object with asphalt markings or curb edges; a nearby moving person or vehicle nearby

qa.evidence_note: the car remains stationary among similar parked vehicles

qa.question: Across the EO scale or angle change, which fixed-state cue stays paired with the object?

options.A: cold moving person; view scale or angle changes
options.B: parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles; view scale or angle changes
options.C: asphalt markings or curb edges; view scale or angle changes
options.D: parked car state with residual heat; view scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: the car remains stationary among similar parked vehicles

qa.question: In IR, which contrast pairing is visible near the object?

options.A: engine-area heat and cool body contrast; parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles
options.B: engine-area heat and cool body contrast; cold moving person
options.C: engine-area heat and cool body contrast; parked car state with residual heat
options.D: engine-area heat and cool body contrast; asphalt markings or curb edges

qa.evidence_note: a bright engine-compartment hotspot and a cold moving person near the car appear

qa.question: Which nearby cue creates a same-scene confusion?

options.A: asphalt markings or curb edges; near the object
options.B: parked car state with residual heat; near the object
options.C: parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles; near the object
options.D: cold moving person; near the object

qa.evidence_note: turning residual engine heat or the moving person into vehicle motion

qa.question: Which EO/IR cue pair is visible?

options.A: EO: parked car state with residual heat; IR: asphalt markings or curb edges
options.B: EO: parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles; IR: engine-area heat and cool body contrast
options.C: EO: cold moving person; IR: engine-area heat and cool body contrast
options.D: EO: parked car state with residual heat; IR: engine-area heat and cool body contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: the car remains stationary among similar parked vehicles; IR: a bright engine-compartment hotspot and a cold moving person near the car appear

qa.question: Which cue pairing continues through camera/view change?

options.A: the object: cold moving person; screen scale or angle changes
options.B: the object: asphalt markings or curb edges; screen scale or angle changes
options.C: the object: parked car state with residual heat; screen scale or angle changes
options.D: the object: parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles; screen scale or angle changes

qa.evidence_note: the car remains stationary among similar parked vehicles

qa.question: Which EO cue is aligned with the IR contrast cue?

options.A: EO cue: parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles; IR cue: engine-area heat and cool body contrast
options.B: EO cue: asphalt markings or curb edges; IR cue: engine-area heat and cool body contrast
options.C: EO cue: parked car state with residual heat; IR cue: engine-area heat and cool body contrast
options.D: EO cue: cold moving person; IR cue: engine-area heat and cool body contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: the car remains stationary among similar parked vehicles; IR: a bright engine-compartment hotspot and a cold moving person near the car appear

qa.question: Which competing cue appears in the cross-modal chain?

options.A: EO/visible cue: cold moving person; IR/contrast context: engine-area heat and cool body contrast
options.B: EO/visible cue: parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles; IR/contrast context: engine-area heat and cool body contrast
options.C: EO/visible cue: asphalt markings or curb edges; IR/contrast context: engine-area heat and cool body contrast
options.D: EO/visible cue: parked car state with residual heat; IR/contrast context: engine-area heat and cool body contrast

qa.evidence_note: turning residual engine heat or the moving person into vehicle motion

qa.question: When the view changes, which cue is checked against the nearby alternative?

options.A: cold moving person; parked car state with residual heat
options.B: asphalt markings or curb edges; parked car state with residual heat
options.C: parked car state with residual heat; cold moving person
options.D: parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles; cold moving person

options.A: engine-area heat and cool body contrast; cold moving person
options.B: engine-area heat and cool body contrast; parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles
options.C: engine-area heat and cool body contrast; parked car state with residual heat
options.D: engine-area heat and cool body contrast; asphalt markings or curb edges

qa.evidence_note: a bright engine-compartment hotspot and a cold moving person near the car appear

options.B: EO: asphalt markings or curb edges; IR: engine-area heat and cool body contrast
options.C: EO: parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles; IR: engine-area heat and cool body contrast
options.D: EO: cold moving person; IR: engine-area heat and cool body contrast

qa.evidence_note: EO: the car remains stationary among similar parked vehicles; IR: a bright engine-compartment hotspot and a cold moving person near the car appear

options.B: the object: parking-lot lines and nearby parked vehicles; nearby cue: parked car state with residual heat
options.C: the object: parked car state with residual heat; nearby cue: cold moving person

group_scoring_recommendation.C1: Use squared-ratio consistency scoring for the eight-step parallel cue chain.
group_scoring_recommendation.H1: Prefer prefix scoring because the four questions form a cue-chain verdict.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, evidence_note, rationales, or time_reference metadata to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v15 text-gate repair r2: options are neutral cue-pair choices with same-scene distractors, reducing answerability from wording alone.
acceptance_gates: Run request key-path leakage audit after export.
Run Qwen text-only/no-video baseline before video inference.
Reject samples or templates whose text-only accuracy suggests wording shortcuts.
Keep evidence_note fields internal; they are for annotation audit only.
